_comment: Rotating educational cards shown in the 'Did You Know?' feed. Grounded in WHO harm-reduction guidance and general public-health knowledge; should be reviewed and updated by an NDA/CIDAZ clinical partner before production use.

cards.text_en: WHO: Stopping guka suddenly without medical support can cause seizures. Withdrawal from opioid-type substances should be medically supervised.
cards.text_sn: WHO: Kurega guka pakarepo pasina rubatsiro rwezvokwadi kunogona kukonzera kufofa. Kurega kunofanira kuve nerubatsiro rwechiremba.

cards.text_en: Inhalant misuse can cause a fatal heart reaction the very first time it's used — there's no such thing as a 'safe' first try.
cards.text_sn: Kunhuwidzana kwezvinhu kunogona kukonzera dambudziko remwoyo rinouraya kunyangwe pakuedza kwekutanga.

cards.text_en: Mixing alcohol with cough syrup (codeine) increases the risk of dangerously slowed breathing — this combination is more dangerous than either substance alone.
cards.text_sn: Kusanganisa doro nemutoriro kunowedzera njodzi yekufema zvishoma-shoma — izvi zvine njodzi kupfuura kushandisa chimwe chete.

cards.text_en: Talking to a trained counsellor is confidential. Childline Zimbabwe's 116 line does not automatically report your name to anyone.
cards.text_sn: Kutaura nemupi weruzivo akadzidziswa hakuzivikanwe. Nhare ye116 yeChildline Zimbabwe haitumire zita rako kwaani zvake.

cards.text_en: The Friendship Bench model started in Mbare, Harare, and uses trained community lay-counsellors to offer free talk therapy on park benches — proven effective in peer-reviewed research.
cards.text_sn: Friendship Bench yakatanga kuMbare, Harare, uye inoshandisa vanhu vakadzidziswa munharaunda kupa kurapwa kwemazwi pachena.

cards.text_en: Street mixtures sold under names like 'pink' often contain an unpredictable combination of substances — you genuinely cannot know what's in them.
cards.text_sn: Zvakasanganiswa zvemumigwagwa senge 'pink' kazhinji hazvizivikanwe zviri mukati — hausi kuziva chaizvo.

cards.text_en: Recovery is possible. Substance dependence is treatable, and reaching out early generally leads to better outcomes than waiting.
cards.text_sn: Kubva mukushandisa mishonga kunogoneka. Kutsvaga rubatsiro nekukurumidza kazhinji kunobatsira kupfuura kumirira.

cards.text_en: Stigma is one of the biggest reasons young people avoid seeking help. A calm, non-judgemental conversation opens more doors than a lecture does.
cards.text_sn: Kunyadziswa ndicho chimwe chezvikonzero zvikuru zvinoita kuti vechidiki vasatsvage rubatsiro. Hurukuro dzinyoro dzinovhura mikova kupfuura kutsiura.
